prose: Fukui reactivity indices were computed for phenol (13 atoms: O, C×6, H×6) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used doublet open-shell SCFs (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme in PySCF. The geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied phenol.xyz without re-optimization. All three SCFs converged; charge conservation checks passed (sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000). The headline value reported is the maximum f+ index (most electrophilic site): Atom 3 (C, ortho ring carbon), f+ = 2.1028319. The most nucleophilic site is Atom 1 (O, hydroxyl oxygen), f− = 0.16201477. The dual descriptor confirms ortho/para electrophilic character of ring carbons and nucleophilic character of the oxygen, consistent with the known ortho/para-directing effect of the hydroxyl group. No warnings were reported. integrity.trustworthy = true. Method: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard), gas phase, Mulliken charges, PySCF, chemkit fukui-reactivity skill.
